title: use imagecaches for local tests
body:
when doing localized tests (not varlink), we can use secondary image
stores as read-only image caches.  this cuts down on test time
significantly because each test does not need to restore the images from
a tarball.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
it looks like the imagecache technique cuts the integration tests in roughly half.
Awesome, and now we get some nice tests of additional-stores.

*lgtm* (lowercase, since I don't fully understand everything here)  :smile: 

Q: Why all the repetition of `podmanTest.SeedImages()` -  I thought the "seeding" was taken care of by "SynchronizedBeforeSuite"?  Perhaps renaming `SeedImages` to something like `ResetImageCache` would be more precise?

Restarted the failing tests.

For reference, we were getting ERRNO 0x11 out of cp_test.go:114 - which seems to be the flake that we were seeing last week, when things were bad, except we have a cause now (EEXIST, seemingly)
